2019/015.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project studies household saving behaviour using the Wealth and Assets Survey; Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding saving behaviour' indicates characterising patterns and profiles of saving rather than testing a policy or exposure; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2019/024.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning and Migration & Demographics because the title concerns the Right to Buy scheme, gentrification, and migration/residential population change using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the Right to Buy is a named housing policy whose consequences are being analysed; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2020/003.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning, Labour Market & Employment and Public Finance & Taxation because the project studies spatial sorting in housing and employment via planning decisions, housing subsidies, public investments and business rates; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the title explicitly assesses the impact of named policy instruments; no tag because the COVID-19 dataset appears instrumental rather than a central lens of the research question.

2020/006.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the outcome is educational inequality and disadvantage; Outcome Tracking because parental separation is a naturally occurring family circumstance linked to downstream educational outcomes in the ONS Longitudinal Study; Demographic disparities / equity tag because comparison of 'diverging destinies' across three cohorts is central to the research question.

2020/013.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project analyses firm-level research and development using BERD, the Business Structure Database, patents and innovation surveys; Descriptive Monitoring because the title indicates characterising firm-level R&D activity rather than evaluating a named intervention; no tag because the COVID-linked dataset does not make pandemic conditions central to the stated question.

2020/019.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Public Finance & Taxation because the project examines how government expenditure affects employment and wages using firm and earnings data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the analytical operation estimates the impact of deliberate public spending; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens appears in the entry.

2020/022.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Labour Market & Employment because the project analyses digital skills requirements within English apprenticeship standards and occupational job roles; Descriptive Monitoring because it profiles skills content across job roles rather than testing an exposure or evaluating an intervention; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2020/027.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project concerns COVID-19 infection, immunisation and death outcomes via the Covid-19 Infection Survey and linked mortality data; Descriptive Monitoring because infection surveillance measures prevalence, levels and trends in the population; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic and infection surveillance are the explicit central condition of the study.

2020/030/b.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because occupational mobility is central to the research question; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because the ONS Longitudinal Study is used to follow individuals' occupational movements over extended periods; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2020/030/a.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm engagement in design and exporting behaviour using the UK Innovation Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring firm characteristic (design engagement) to a downstream outcome (exporting); no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens applies.

2020/033.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the title centres on education in relation to identity; Outcome Tracking because the framing links educational status as a naturally occurring characteristic to identity outcomes using Labour Force Survey data; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic lens is explicit in the entry.

2020/045.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics and Public Finance & Taxation because the title studies migration and its relationship with redistributive local authority spending; Outcome Tracking because migration is a naturally occurring exposure linked to a spending outcome; no tag because demographic-group comparison and pandemic framing are not central.

2020/046.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies wage and employment dynamics using ASHE, New Earnings Survey and linked earnings-census data; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because 'dynamics' with long-run linked earnings panels indicates following workers' wage and employment trajectories over time; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2020/050.rationale: Assigned Unclear from Register Entry because 'Inequalities in the 21st Century' with the general-purpose ONS Longitudinal Study does not resolve which substantive outcome (health, employment, income) is studied; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising patterns of inequality over time; demographic disparities tag because comparing outcomes across population groups is central to an inequalities study on census-linked data.

2020/053.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firms' decisions to become multinational using business surveys, FDI and R&D data; Outcome Tracking because it links naturally occurring domestic economic incentives to the firm-level outcome of multinational status and its effects; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens applies.

2020/056.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because adherence to stay-at-home public health guidance is a public health behaviour question; Outcome Tracking because the study analyses factors associated with adherence rather than building a prospective prediction tool; COVID-19 tag because pandemic guidance and its social impacts are the explicit condition of the research.

2020/062.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because productivity and inclusivity trade-offs are studied with the Business Structure Database, ASHE, APS and BRES covering firm and workforce outcomes; Outcome Tracking because local institutions are analysed as an exposure shaping productivity, sustainability and inclusivity outcomes; no tag because demographic comparison and pandemic framing are not central.

2020/066.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the CMA taskforce concerns markets and business behaviour, using the Business Impact of COVID-19 Survey; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the project explicitly evaluates a named institutional intervention (the CMA Covid-19 taskforce); COVID-19 tag because the taskforce and datasets are pandemic-specific.

2020/074.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the health of older people is the substantive object, studied via the ONS Longitudinal Study; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising older people's health across places as an asset; no tag because older age defines the study population rather than a demographic comparison, and no pandemic lens is present.

2020/077.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because place of death in older adults is an end-of-life care outcome studied via the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because social determinants are naturally occurring exposures linked to the downstream outcome of place of death; no tag because the determinants are socioeconomic rather than a central demographic-group comparison and no pandemic framing appears.

2020/078.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the study examines health and mortality outcomes of postal workers using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because it links an occupational status (postal work) to downstream health and mortality outcomes; no tag because no pandemic or demographic-equity lens is central.

2020/082.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because wellbeing is the substantive outcome measured via the Coronavirus social impacts survey; Descriptive Monitoring because the project charts changes over time and variation across groups rather than testing a specific exposure-outcome design; COVID-19 & Pandemic because the pandemic is the central condition, and Demographic disparities because comparison by demographic characteristics is explicit in the title.

2020/083.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because labour market outcomes are the substantive object over 1991-2011; Outcome Tracking because religion is a naturally occurring characteristic linked to employment outcomes; Demographic disparities tag because comparison across religious groups is central to the research question.

2020/084.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because excess mortality is analysed as a health outcome using death registration data; Descriptive Monitoring because the aim is to measure levels and trends of excess deaths during and after the pandemic; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic is the explicit framing of the mortality analysis.

2020/086.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment, Business & Productivity and Migration & Demographics because the project studies a labour supply shock from immigration policy and its effects on firm outcomes using earnings, business and labour force datasets; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the exposure is a deliberate UK immigration policy whose effects are estimated; no tag because migrant status comparison and pandemic framing are not central.

2020/092.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics because fertility is the substantive demographic object, and Data Infrastructure & Methodology because improving fertility forecasting methodologies is itself an object of the project; Descriptive Monitoring because it characterises recent UK fertility patterns and Methodological / Infrastructure Research because it develops forecasting methods; no tag because no pandemic or equity lens is evident.

2020/095.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because firm productivity and trade are the substantive outcomes analysed with business and trade datasets; Outcome Tracking because policy uncertainty is a naturally occurring economic condition linked to firm outcomes rather than a named intervention; no tag because no pandemic or demographic lens applies.

2020/104.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the object is COVID-19 infection in schools using the Schools Infection Survey linked to immunisation and Test & Trace data, with schools as the setting rather than an educational outcome; Descriptive Monitoring because the analysis measures infection levels to inform government decisions; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic surveillance is the explicit focus.

2020/106.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because non-coronavirus death registrations are analysed as health outcomes; Descriptive Monitoring because the project profiles patterns of deaths over January-November 2020 without testing a specific exposure; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic period and the distinction from coronavirus deaths frame the analysis.

2020/118.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because multimorbidity, polypharmacy, health, resilience and wellbeing in the Welsh population are the substantive objects; Risk Prediction / Early Identification because machine learning is used to discover phenotypes that identify groups at elevated risk of poorer outcomes; no tag because no pandemic or demographic-equity comparison is central.

2020/120.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Labour Market & Employment because the project profiles COVID-19 infection, deaths (Infection Survey, death registrations) alongside employment and earnings data (APS, ASHE) at local-area level; Descriptive Monitoring because 'Local Area Profiles' indicates characterising levels and patterns across places rather than testing an exposure-outcome design; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic is the explicit subject of the profiles.

2020/127.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project studies the health of domiciliary care workers and care service planning and carer support; Outcome Tracking because it establishes the impact of the naturally occurring COVID-19 pandemic on worker health outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19's impact is the central condition of the research question.

2021/002.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project examines coronavirus social impacts on wellbeing and daily life using the ONS social impacts survey; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising the social impacts on the London population rather than testing an exposure-outcome relationship; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because coronavirus impacts are explicitly the focus of the title.

2021/012.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project seeks epidemiological insights into COVID-19 infection, drawing on the COVID-19 Infection Survey and linked census-death data; Descriptive Monitoring because 'investigating epidemiological insights across the UK' indicates measuring infection patterns and distribution rather than evaluating an intervention; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 infection is the explicit research object.

2021/013.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because COVID-19 mortality is analysed as a health outcome using death registrations and linked census-death data; Outcome Tracking because the design links ethnicity, socioeconomic position and geography as exposures to mortality outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic and Demographic disparities tags because the pandemic frames the research and ethnicity-based inequality in mortality is central to the question.

2021/015.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment, Business & Productivity and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because the project studies economic impacts on both residents and businesses using LFS, BICS and Effects of Tax and Benefits data; Outcome Tracking because it links the naturally occurring pandemic to downstream economic outcomes rather than evaluating a named policy; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic economic impact is the explicit research question.

2021/017.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project concerns medicines manufacturing firms using business surveys (ABS, BERD, BSD); Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it evaluates the named ISCF Medicines Manufacturing programme; no tag because neither pandemic conditions nor demographic comparison is central.

2021/018.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies impacts on working lives using the Labour Force Survey and BICS; Outcome Tracking because it links the naturally occurring pandemic to labour-market outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic and Demographic disparities tags because the pandemic is the central exposure and the comparison between women's and men's working lives is central to the question.

2021/028.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project concerns healthcare resource allocation and unmet health need, using mortality registrations; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the analytical operation is developing an approach to account for unmet need within allocation formulas; no tag because equity here concerns need-based allocation rather than a demographic-group comparison or pandemic lens.

2021/032.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies non-monetary work benefits and the gender pay gap using ASHE earnings data; Descriptive Monitoring because the operation is measuring and characterising the pay gap's composition when non-monetary benefits are included; Demographic disparities tag because the gender comparison in pay is central to the research question.

2021/040.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the project studies serious and organised crime cases in Crown Courts using MoJ court and prisoner datasets; Descriptive Monitoring and Service Interaction / Systems Analysis because it examines the nature and extent of cases and their outcomes as they progress through courts and custodial journeys; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/044.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Labour Market & Employment because the project links occupation to COVID-19 infection using the Covid-19 Infection Survey; Outcome Tracking because occupation is a naturally occurring exposure linked to infection outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 infection is the central outcome.

2021/046.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project concerns palliative and end of life care using death registrations and health research data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis and Methodological / Infrastructure Research because it evaluates the pandemic response in palliative care while explicitly connecting and boosting data assets; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because the pandemic response is the object of evaluation.

2021/053.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because occupational change and skill mismatch among graduates are central, using Labour Force Survey and APS data; Outcome Tracking because COVID-19 is a naturally occurring exposure linked to occupational and mismatch outcomes; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic effects frame the research question.

2021/054.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the distributional impact assessment of a rail scheme draws on the Annual Population Survey to assess impacts on population and labour-market characteristics; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Northern Powerhouse Rail is a specific named infrastructure scheme being assessed; Demographic disparities tag because a distributional impact assessment centrally compares impacts across population groups.

2021/071.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because poverty and inequality in Central London are the substantive focus; Descriptive Monitoring and Outcome Tracking because the project profiles poverty and inequality and examines how the pandemic affected them; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic impact is explicitly central to the question.

2021/087.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics and Labour Market & Employment because spatial (residential/population) mobility and social mobility are the substantive objects, using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because the design follows individuals across time to trace mobilities shaping geographical inequalities; no tag because the inequality lens is geographic rather than demographic and no pandemic framing is present.

2021/089.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because scam victimisation is the substantive focus, using the Crime Survey for England and Wales; Outcome Tracking because victimisation is a naturally occurring exposure linked to subjective wellbeing outcomes; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2021/090.rationale: Assigned Environment & Agriculture and Data Infrastructure & Methodology because the project builds linkage between individual and farm-level data for agricultural research; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because developing the linked data asset is the central analytical operation; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/104.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the self-employed and the labour-market impacts of the pandemic are the substantive focus, using Labour Force Survey and APS data; Outcome Tracking because COVID-19 is a naturally occurring exposure linked to outcomes for the self-employed; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because pandemic impacts are central, and Demographic disparities tag because the explicit inclusivity framing centres comparison of impacts across social groups.

2021/114.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care and Environment & Agriculture because the project studies health and well-being impacts of air quality interventions in urban environments; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because Low Emission Zones and Clean Air Zones are named policy interventions whose impacts are being estimated; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/123.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the 'People research strand' of the Economy 2030 Inquiry draws heavily on earnings, employment, and vacancy surveys (ASHE, LFS, APS, Vacancy Survey) indicating a workforce focus; Descriptive Monitoring because an inquiry strand characterising the state and trends of the labour market is the visible operation; no tag because the coronavirus dataset appears as one of many contextual sources rather than a central pandemic lens.

2021/131.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because precarious employment among men is the substantive object, using Labour Force Survey data; Descriptive Monitoring because the analysis profiles the prevalence and patterning of precarious employment across groups; Demographic disparities tag because ethnicity-based comparison is central to the research question.

2021/132.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because foreign market power and its role in the UK economy is a firm/industrial-structure question, supported by firm-level datasets (ARD, BSD, trade-linked IDBR); Outcome Tracking because the study links foreign market power as a naturally occurring economic condition to downstream economic outcomes; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is present.

2021/137.rationale: Assigned Data Infrastructure & Methodology because the Social Value Bank is itself a measurement and valuation framework being produced from survey data (Crime Survey, Understanding Society); Methodological / Infrastructure Research because developing and maintaining this valuation asset is the main analytical operation; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2021/138.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project maps the industrial and economic structure of the Shetland Islands economy using business surveys; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because constructing input-output tables is the development of a statistical data asset; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/140.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Health & Social Care because wages, hours of work, and income are analysed in relation to well-being as a central outcome; Outcome Tracking because naturally occurring labour-market circumstances are linked to a downstream well-being outcome; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic framing appears in the entry.

2021/154.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because occupational sorting by task content and language skills is the substantive object, using Census data; Descriptive Monitoring because the analysis characterises patterns of occupational sorting across groups rather than testing a policy or single exposure-outcome chain; Demographic disparities tag because comparison of immigrant and UK-born ethnic minorities versus White British is central to the question.

2021/172.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the UK labour market is the substantive object, evidenced by ASHE and LFS datasets; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the project estimates the impact of institutional change, a deliberate institutional/policy shift, on labour-market outcomes; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is central.

2021/176.rationale: Assigned Environment & Agriculture because the project concerns sustainable and circular energy transitions on Scottish Islands using meter-level gas and electricity consumption data; Descriptive Monitoring because measuring and characterising local energy consumption patterns is the visible analytical operation; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2021/180.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm innovation and export performance using the Longitudinal Small Business Survey; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring firm characteristic (innovation) to a downstream outcome (export performance); no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic framing is present.

2022/022.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because birth outcomes are a health outcome, and Crime & Justice because the study population is defined by the women's prison estate; Descriptive Monitoring because the project measures births and birth outcomes within this population using birth registrations; no tag because neither demographic-group comparison nor pandemic framing is central.

2022/041.rationale: Assigned Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because digital poverty concerns material deprivation and access to resources; Descriptive Monitoring because the project profiles the prevalence and distribution of digital poverty across the UK using surveys like LFS and Opinions and Lifestyle; no tag because the coronavirus dataset appears contextual rather than a central pandemic lens.

2022/044.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project studies entrepreneurial firms and employment responses to the China trade shock using business and labour force surveys; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring trade exposure to firm and employment outcomes rather than a named policy; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2022/045.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because creative-industry clusters, spillovers, and growth potential are firm and industry-level phenomena studied via business, R&D, and innovation surveys; Outcome Tracking because the analysis links cluster presence (a naturally occurring condition) to spillover and growth outcomes; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic framing is evident.

2022/047.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because children's academic and behavioural outcomes are the studied object, and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because Universal Credit is a benefit affecting household resources; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the project estimates effects of a named welfare reform (Universal Credit); no tag because no demographic-group comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2022/060.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project concerns business support and firm outcomes using the Business Structure Database and ASHE; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it explicitly evaluates the ERDF business-support programme; no tag because neither demographic disparity nor pandemic framing is present.

2022/073.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the disadvantage attainment gap in the Growing Up in England data is the central outcome; Outcome Tracking because the project links naturally occurring parental education and occupation to children's attainment; no tag because the comparison is socioeconomic rather than across a demographic or equality-relevant group.

2022/095.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project examines firm-level productivity dynamics using business surveys and the Business Structure Database; Descriptive Monitoring because 'micro-anatomy' indicates decomposing and characterising the productivity slowdown rather than testing a policy or exposure; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2022/109.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because startups and firm activity across regions are the substantive object, studied via the Business Structure Database and Annual Business Survey; Descriptive Monitoring because the project characterises regional patterns of startup activity and inequality rather than evaluating a named intervention; no tag because regional inequality is not a demographic or equality-relevant group comparison and no pandemic framing is present.

2022/117.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies access and representation in elite higher education using UCAS admissions data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is profiling who accesses and is represented within elite institutions; Demographic disparities / equity tag because representation and access comparisons across groups are central to the research question.

2022/129.rationale: Assigned Data Infrastructure & Methodology and Environment & Agriculture because the project builds a linkage between individual and farm-level data for agricultural research using the Agricultural Research Collection; Methodological / Infrastructure Research because the central operation is creating and developing a linked data asset (AD|ARC); no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/132.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project models Northern Ireland trade using business inquiry and sales/exports statistics, with firm trade activity as the object; Descriptive Monitoring because the trade model characterises the level and pattern of NI trade flows rather than testing a named intervention; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/133.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project analyses earnings differentials using the Earnings and Employees Study; Descriptive Monitoring because it measures and characterises the earnings gap between groups; Demographic disparities / equity tag because the Catholic-Protestant (religious group) comparison is the core of the research question.

2022/135.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because the project studies wage inequality using the Annual Survey of Hours and Earnings; Outcome Tracking because it links naturally occurring exposures (automation and regulatory conditions) to wage outcomes rather than evaluating a single named policy; no tag because inequality here is socioeconomic/occupational rather than a demographic equality comparison.

2022/136.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because firm entry and exit and aggregate productivity are the substantive objects, using the Annual Business Survey and Annual Respondents Database; Outcome Tracking because the design links firm entry/exit dynamics to downstream productivity outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/144.rationale: Assigned Housing & Planning and Labour Market & Employment because the project studies how the housing market shapes the spatial allocation of credit-constrained workers, combining earnings data with Understanding Society; Outcome Tracking because housing-market conditions are analysed as an exposure affecting worker location and labour outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/159.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment, Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards and Health & Social Care because the project jointly analyses paid employment, deprivation and unpaid care work using the ONS Longitudinal Study; Outcome Tracking because it links unpaid caring circumstances to employment and deprivation outcomes across England and Wales; no tag because no central demographic-equality comparison or pandemic framing is stated.

2022/172.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies innovation, management capability and productivity in SMEs using firm-level surveys (ABS, BERD, UK Innovation Survey, Management Practices Survey); Outcome Tracking because it links firm characteristics such as management capability and innovation to productivity outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/176.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project examines how local skill deficiencies affect firm performance, productivity and agglomeration using employer skills surveys, business surveys and the Labour Force Survey; Outcome Tracking because skill deficiencies are analysed as an exposure affecting downstream firm and local economic outcomes; no tag because no equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/177.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the title concerns firm innovation and technology management and the datasets are firm-level business, R&D, productivity and innovation surveys; Unclear from Register Entry for purpose because the title states only a topic and neither title nor datasets reveal whether the design is monitoring, evaluation, or exposure-outcome analysis; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2022/193.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the project studies reoffending using MoJ courts, prisons and probation data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it tests whether the enforced alcohol abstinence intervention reduces reoffending; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is central.

2023/004.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Health & Social Care because the study examines educational pathways and outcomes for children who are looked after, making both education and the children's social care system central; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis and Outcome Tracking because it follows pathways over time and links care status to educational outcomes; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is central.

2023/008.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because elite mobility studied via the Labour Force Survey concerns occupational and social mobility within the labour market; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because mobility into elite positions involves following progression and trajectories; no tag because no central demographic comparison or pandemic framing is evident.

2023/009.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm performance, trade and FDI using extensive firm-level business and trade datasets; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it assesses the impact of Brexit, a specific institutional change, on firm outcomes; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2023/031.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because monopsony and minimum wages concern wages, labour demand and employer power, using earnings and labour force data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the minimum wage is a named policy whose effects are being analysed; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is central.

2023/033.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies the higher education market and university admissions using UCAS data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because the focus on regulation and competition in the HE market indicates analysis of deliberate regulatory and institutional arrangements; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/040.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because domestic violence victimisation, measured via the Crime Survey for England and Wales, is the outcome of interest; Outcome Tracking because the design links a naturally occurring condition (female councillor representation) to domestic violence outcomes rather than evaluating a named policy; no tag because gender enters as a political representation exposure rather than a central demographic outcome comparison.

2023/046.rationale: Assigned Migration & Demographics because immigrants' location choices and residential settlement patterns are the substantive object; Outcome Tracking because the study links a naturally occurring exposure (local far-right support) to migrants' location decisions; no tag because migrant status defines the study population rather than a comparative demographic-equity lens.

2023/056.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because children's social services involvement in Wales is the substantive system studied; Service Interaction / Systems Analysis and Descriptive Monitoring because the project examines patterns of contact with and outcomes from children's social services across groups; Demographic disparities / equity tag because ethnic and religious inequalities are the explicit focus of the research question.

2023/066.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the project studies vaccination uptake using COVID-19 Infection Survey, vaccination and Test and Trace data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is measuring heterogeneity in vaccination across social networks rather than testing an exposure-outcome relationship or evaluating a policy; COVID-19 & Pandemic tag because COVID-19 vaccination and infection surveillance are the central condition of the study.

2023/073.rationale: Assigned Crime & Justice because the project concerns domestic abuse proceedings and private/public family justice using MoJ FamilyMan family court data; Service Interaction / Systems Analysis because the focus is on overlap and pathways of cases through the family court system; no tag because no demographic-comparison or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/094.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because the research object is health outcomes of Scottish nurses; Outcome Tracking because it links a naturally occurring circumstance (deprivation) to downstream health outcomes using Census 2011; no tag because deprivation is a socioeconomic rather than demographic/equality comparison and no pandemic framing appears.

2023/098.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills and Health & Social Care because the project links time in social care (Children Looked After, Children in Need) to educational attainment at ages 16-18; Outcome Tracking because it connects a naturally occurring circumstance (time in care) to a downstream attainment outcome rather than evaluating a named intervention; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is central.

2023/113.rationale: Assigned Health & Social Care because later-life health and morbidity are the research outcomes; Outcome Tracking and Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis because early-life health and nutritional environment are linked to later-life outcomes across the life span using the ONS Longitudinal Study; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic condition is central.

2023/114.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment and Education & Skills because the project studies how compulsory education affects occupational sorting and earnings stability using ASHE and LFS data; Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because compulsory education is a deliberate policy whose impact is being estimated; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/167.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Environment & Agriculture because the project measures firms' market power along the milk supply chain, an agricultural sector, using firm-level Annual Respondents Database data; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is measuring and characterising market power rather than testing an exposure-outcome relationship or evaluating a named policy; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens appears.

2023/170.rationale: Assigned Public Finance & Taxation and Poverty, Wealth & Living Standards because tax and benefit microsimulation concerns fiscal transfers and household resources using household income and expenditure surveys; Methodological / Infrastructure Research and Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because microsimulation involves developing and maintaining a modelling tool to simulate the distributional impacts of tax and benefit policies; no tag because no demographic-equity comparison or pandemic condition is central.

2023/173.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because the project studies grade inflation, attainment, and subsequent outcomes at UK universities using Longitudinal Education Outcomes data, with later outcomes used to study consequences of education; Descriptive Monitoring and Outcome Tracking because it profiles trends in grade inflation and links university attainment to downstream outcomes; no tag because no demographic comparison or pandemic framing is central.

2023/181.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the project assesses Enterprise Zones' effects on firms (Business Structure Database, Annual Business Survey) and on earnings and employment (ASHE, LFS); Policy Evaluation / Impact Analysis because it evaluates the impact of a specific named policy, UK Enterprise Zones; no tag because no demographic-equity or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/190.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity and Labour Market & Employment because the title centres on productivity from a macroeconomic perspective using earnings and labour-force datasets (ASHE, LFS); Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding productivity' indicates characterising levels and patterns rather than testing a named intervention; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is evident.

2023/204.rationale: Assigned Business & Productivity because the project studies firm-level R&D, innovation and productivity using business surveys (ABS, BERD, UK Innovation Survey); Descriptive Monitoring because 'understanding' firm-level patterns in a region indicates profiling rather than evaluating a named policy; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is present.

2023/205.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because educational attainment is the substantive outcome, using Growing Up in England and school census data; Outcome Tracking because the design links family background (a naturally occurring circumstance) to attainment outcomes; Demographic disparities tag because comparison 'across ethnic categories' is central to the research question.

2023/212.rationale: Assigned Education & Skills because school exclusion and subsequent education-to-work outcomes are central, using LEO linked education-employment data; Life-Course / Trajectory Analysis and Outcome Tracking because the project follows young people's trajectories and links the exclusion experience to downstream outcomes; no tag because no demographic or pandemic lens is central.

2023/215.rationale: Assigned Labour Market & Employment because labour-market transitions of over-50s are the substantive focus, using longitudinal LFS datasets; Descriptive Monitoring because the visible operation is characterising transition patterns over short LFS panels rather than testing an intervention or extended life-course trajectories; no tag because over-50s defines the study population rather than a central demographic comparison.
